summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a late‑’72 powerhouse show highlighted by a monster Dark Star → Morning Dew and tight band playing
review: The performance is widely praised for its high energy, deep improvisations and standout moments – especially the 28‑minute Dark Star that segues into a stellar Morning Dew, plus superb renditions of Bird Song, Playing in the Band, Deal and Promised Land. The soundboard source is described as clear and well balanced (with only minor mix quirks), making the show highly listenable.

ai_rating.rationale: All five reviews are 4‑5 stars and emphasize strong musical performance with many standout songs; recording is consistently described as clear and well‑balanced, yielding an overall excellent rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, balanced mix; a few comments about Bob's guitar being forward and Phil sitting high in the mix, plus a tiny cut in the Dark Star, but no major issues.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The first set builds nicely from early‑show energy (Bertha, Me & My Uncle) into a high‑spirited middle; the second set launches with an intense Playing in the Band that feeds into a primal Dark Star → Morning Dew and finishes strong with rock‑heavy Deal, Promised Land and a double OMSN/Casey Jones finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as sweet; guitar runs solid though less wah‑wah than other ’72 shows; wails on Bird Song and Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass sits high in the mix, delivers a cool solo during Dark Star and dominates the Morning Dew section.
band_member_comments.Bob: Strong rhythm chords, occasional forward placement in the mix, funny high note on Big Railroad, overall solid contribution.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is highlighted as firmly rooted, delivering some of his best solos (Big River, Jack Straw) and shining throughout the set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill’s drumming described as ferocious, driving the second‑set intensity.
